description.p: Gator is a modern GTK4/Libadwaita frontend for the croc command-line tool, making secure peer-to-peer file transfers simple and elegant.

ul.li: Send files, folders, and text snippets with a simple drag-and-drop interface
Receive transfers using codes or by scanning QR images
Adaptive layout that works on desktop and mobile devices
Built-in QR code generation for easy sharing
Full integration with GNOME and other desktop environments

description.p: All transfers are end-to-end encrypted and work across different networks and platforms. No registration or third-party accounts required.

screenshot.caption: Send files, folders, and text with drag-and-drop simplicity
Receive transfers using codes or QR scanning

ul.li: Progress bars for send/receive (croc carriage-return output)
Empty croc defaults so Gator matches plain croc relays and flags
Sent-file checkmarks and clearer transfer-finished feedback
GSettings schema fixes; bundled croc in Flatpak

description.p: Updated to the freshest GNOME 50 runtime and corresponding SDK extension (25.08) for latest libadwaita and platform features.
This release fixes several critical bugs and improves stability:

ul.li: Fixed thread-safety issues in subprocess handling
Unified global flag construction for send and receive
Added proper error logging throughout the application
Improved HIG compliance with libadwaita dialogs
Better handling of QR generation and scanning errors
